text: Managing a team effectively requires a mix of skills, but communication is the foundation. It’s important to set clear goals and hold regular check-ins, so everyone knows their role. Delegation also matters: you can’t do everything yourself, so trust your team members with tasks that match their strengths. After projects, give constructive feedback to help them grow, and remember to recognize good work—this boosts motivation. Conflicts will happen, so address them quickly by listening to all sides and finding a fair solution. A team that feels heard and supported works better together.
